dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9249 IH: No PLA Employees Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moolenaar (for himself and Mr. Cline) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide for a limitation on approval of an immigrant petition on behalf of an alien who was previously employed by an entity on the Department of Defense’s 1260H list.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No PLA Employees Act of 2026.

section.header: Inadmissibility and Deportability Related to Current or Previous Employment by a Chinese Military Company Or Foreign Institution Engaged in Problematic Activity

subsection.text: Section 212(a)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1182(a)(2)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.header: Current or Previous Employment by a Chinese Military Company
subparagraph.text: Any alien who is currently or was previously employed by an entity identified under section 1260H of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021 is inadmissible.

subparagraph.header: Current or Previous Employment by a Foreign Institution Engaged in Problematic Activity
subparagraph.text: Any alien who is currently or was previously employed by an entity identified under section 1286 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019 is inadmissible.

subsection.text: Section 237(a)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1227(a)(2)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.header: Current or Previous Employment by a Chinese Military Company
subparagraph.text: Any alien who is currently or was previously employed by an entity identified under section 1260H of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021 is deportable.

subparagraph.header: Current or Previous Employment by a Foreign Institution Engaged in Problematic Activity
subparagraph.text: Any alien who is currently or was previously employed by an entity identified under section 1286 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019 is deportable.
